title: practical english usage - Part 86
content:
3 -ing    form    or  infinitive -ing Infinitives,        forms     and   Past   Participles       after    Nouns,    Verbs,   etc    Section 9 grammar •  105  infinitives or -ing forms: both possible with different uses We regret to say that we are unable to help you.

We regret to inform passengers that the 14.50 train is one hour late.

Regret + infinitive is used mostly in announcements of bad news.

1 regret leaving school at 16 - it was a big mistake.

one did.

Regret + -ing form looks back at the past - at something that one is sorry that 3 regret She stopped talking about that and went on to describe her other problems.

Go on + infinitive refers to a change of activity.

She went on talking about her illness until we all went to sleep.

Go on + -ing form means 'continue'.

I forgot to buy the soap.

You must remember to fetch Mr Lewis from the station tomorrow.

or still had to do at the moment of remembering or forgetting.

Remember/forget + infinitive looks forward in time - at things that one still has I'll never forget meeting the Queen.

I still remember buying my first bicycle.

expressions with similar meanings.

Forget.. .ing is used mostly in the phrase I'll never forget.. .ing, and Remember/forget + -ing form looks back at the past - at things that one did.

1  remember           and   forget With some of these verbs and adjectives, there is a difference of meaning.

She was proud of having / to have won.

I started playing / to play the violin when I was 10.

(►   100.4).

Some verbs and adjectives can be followed by either -ing forms or infinitives different       uses 105 infinitives       or  -ing    forms:    both    possible      with For time clauses with on + -ing form, ►     1 15.6.

For -ing forms after conjunctions (e.g.  When planning a trip              115.6.

For the difference between used to + infinitive and be used to + -ing form, ►    87.

Can you arrange for us to get tickets? (     ... for our getting tickets?) not We're still waiting for her to arrive, (     .. .-waiting forker^amving/) not usually possible in these cases.

Note that some verbs are followed by for + object + infinitive. An -ing form is 3 object     + infinitive       after    for:   for    her    to   arrive grammar •  105  infinitives or -ing forms: both possible with different uses Mr Garland teaches skiing in the winter.

She goes to college twice ɑ  week to learn coding.

mostly when we  are referring to lessons or subjects of study.

These verbs (and others with similar meanings) are followed by -ing forms 8  learn      and   teach I don't think she means to get married for the moment.

In the sense of 'intend', mean is followed by an infinitive.

If you want to pass the exam, it will mean studying hard.

-ing form.

Mean in the sense of 'involve', ‘ have as a result' (►  526) can be followed by an 7 mean the wheel. . .) or I tried to change the wheel, but my hands were too cold. (   I tried changing try + infinitive or try + -ing.

To  talk about making an effort to do something difficult, we  can use either wouldn't speak to me.

I tried sending her flowers, writing her letters, giving her presents, but she still To  talk about doing something to see what will happen, we  use try + -ing.

For more details,  ►    110 .

I once heard Brendel play all the Beethoven concertos.

- As I passed his house I heard him  practising the piano.

I saw Emily cross the road and disappear into the bank.

- I looked out of the window and saw Emily crossing the road.

infinitive without to.) Compare: seen/heard from beginning to end. (Note that these verbs are followed by the already going on; infinitives usually refer to complete events/actions which  are -ing forms the verbs suggest that one pays attention to events or actions that are infinitive is like the difference between progressive and simple tenses. With After these verbs, the difference between object + -ing form and object + 5 see,   watch       and   hear Passengers are advised to reserve early.

- Early reservation is advised.

Children are forbidden to sing.
